title: Floral Flaky Australian Shrimp Pie
description: A fragrant, low-sodium shrimp pie with a delicate flaky crust, accented by fresh herbs and edible flowers for a splash of colour and floral aroma.

ingredients.name: shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, thawed if frozen

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a 30x20cm rectangle and transfer to a parchment‑lined baking sheet.
Pat the shrimp dry, then toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp lemon zest, 1 tsp thyme, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, and 1 tsp pepper; set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, sauté the diced onion and minced garlic in 1 tbsp olive oil until translucent. Add the diced tomato and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the low‑sodium vegetable broth and allow the mixture to reduce slightly, about 3 minutes. Add the shrimp and cook until just pink, about 2-3 minutes; remove from heat.
Layer the sautéed sauce and shrimp mixture onto the pastry, spreading evenly. Sprinkle the chopped edible flowers over the top for color and aroma.
Fold the pastry edges over to enclose the filling, crimping the edges with a fork. Brush the top with beaten egg to give a golden finish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.
Allow the pie to rest for 5 minutes before slicing; serve warm.
Optional: garnish with a few extra edible flowers or a squeeze of fresh lemon.
